agents.name: adoption-assistant
agents.description: Analyses Claude Code usage in a project workspace and produces personalised next-step recommendations. Identifies which agents and skills are installed but unused, detects gaps, proposes a prioritised adoption roadmap, and guides users on how to publish quality content with the right format. Read-only — never modifies files in the analysed project.

agents.name: archimate-architect
agents.description: Produces ArchiMate target-architecture files (.archimate) from source diagrams (Lucidchart) or structured YAML manifests. Invokes Python generator tooling via Bash. No application code, no tests.

agents.name: architect
agents.description: Analyzes integration architectures, maps system dependencies, and documents integration patterns across IBM APIC, ACE, MQ, and other middleware. Reads source code, configuration, and documentation to produce architecture mappings and design assessments. Does NOT write application code, tests, or deploy.

agents.name: best-practices
agents.description: Reads consuming project repositories to extract patterns, anti-patterns, and insights about how claude-agents is used in practice. Publishes anonymised findings to the claude-agents repo as INSIGHTS.md entries, and proposes library improvements to the backlog. Never modifies the analysed projects.

agents.name: business-analyst
agents.description: Elicits, formalises, and maintains requirements artefacts (user stories, functional specifications, process models). Bridges the Product Owner's strategic intent and the Architect's technical solution. Does NOT write code, architecture diagrams, or deploy infrastructure.

agents.name: community-manager
agents.description: Drives adoption of claude-agents at TCS through structured internal communication. Produces daily tips, weekly focus posts, monthly experience reports, and hackathon briefs. Formats all content for Confluence, Teams, and email. Never writes code.

agents.name: developer
agents.description: Implements features, fixes bugs, and writes production code following SOLID principles and Clean Code practices. Delegates to this agent when code needs to be written, modified, or refactored. Does NOT run tests, deploy, or update documentation.

agents.name: devops
agents.description: Manages CI/CD pipelines and deployment across GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, and Azure targets, plus Docker configurations and cloud provisioning. Delegates to this agent for build pipelines, container setup, cloud provisioning, and release management. Does NOT write application code or tests.

agents.name: documentarian
agents.description: Maintains project documentation, CLAUDE.md context files, architecture decision records, and ensures documentation stays in sync with code changes. Delegates to this agent after features are implemented, when context needs updating, or when architectural decisions need recording. Does NOT write application code or tests.

agents.name: evaluate-contribution
agents.description: Governance agent for the claude-agents library. Evaluates proposed agents, skills, and teams before they are added to the library. Detects duplicates, checks quality and portability, decides the right type (agent vs skill vs team vs patch), and produces a structured approval/rejection report. Automatically triggered by the evaluate-contribution hook when unknown agents or skills are detected in the workspace.

agents.name: library-maintainer
agents.description: Maintains the entire Agentic-Claude library — agent/skill/team definitions, artefact registry, docs, scripts, hooks, and installer tooling. Belongs ONLY to the claude-agents-maintainers. The Agentic-Claude repo IS its workspace (the one exception to the "AI Tools repo is read-only" rule). Recommends improvements to a human and applies only approved changes. Never commits.

agents.name: product-owner-assistant
agents.description: Assists the Product Owner with prioritization, requirement refinement, stakeholder communication, and agent coordination. This agent does NOT write code, tests, or infrastructure. It helps the PO make decisions and delegates work to specialized agents. Use this agent as the default orchestration layer in interactive sessions.

agents.name: tester
agents.description: Creates and runs tests, validates implementations against acceptance criteria, and reports quality metrics. Delegates to this agent when tests need to be written, executed, or when code quality must be validated. Does NOT write production code.

skills.name: agentic-claude-framework
skills.description: Author and govern the Agentic-Claude framework itself: how to add or change agents, skills, and teams; how per-object semver versioning works; how to keep the CLAUDE.md inventory in sync with disk; what the PreToolUse commit-guard enforces; and how plugin/marketplace packaging ties it together. Use when proposing or applying any change to the structure or governance of THIS repository — never to modify a consuming project. Runs inside the claude-agents-maintainers.

skills.name: agile-user-stories
skills.description: Write, split, and refine agile user stories with well-formed acceptance criteria. Applies the Connextra format, INVEST criteria, and MoSCoW prioritisation. Use when building or grooming a product backlog, refining epics into stories, or preparing stories for sprint planning.

skills.name: analyze-adoption
skills.description: Analyse l'état d'adoption de claude-agents dans un workspace projet et génère un rapport de recommandations personnalisées et actionnables.

skills.name: api-and-interface-design
skills.description: Design a new API or evolve an existing one contract-first before writing any code. Use when defining a new REST endpoint, designing an IBM APIC product, specifying request/response shapes, or establishing versioning and backward-compatibility rules. Produces an OpenAPI 3.x spec and a design decision record.

skills.name: arch-c4
skills.description: Author and refresh C4 diagrams (Context, Container, Component, Deployment, Runtime) via Structurizr DSL, and write the DSL-derived tables into arc42 §3 / §5 / §7 as fenced generated blocks. Five modes: context, container, component, deployment, runtime. Use when the user asks for a C4 diagram, system context, container diagram, component diagram, deployment view, or runtime scenario view.

skills.name: archimate-generation
skills.description: End-to-end skill: reads source diagrams (Lucidchart) or a YAML manifest, generates or updates target-architecture.yaml, and runs the single canonical manifest-driven generator (cmdb_to_archimate.archimate_generator) to produce a .archimate file for the Archi modelling tool.

skills.name: architecture-decision-records
skills.description: Write, review, and maintain Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) following the Nygard format. Use when a significant architectural decision is made, revisited, or superseded. Produces artefacts stored in docs/architecture/decisions/.

skills.name: azure-archimate
skills.description: Represent an Azure estate as ArchiMate views: a management-group / subscription hierarchy view (org-chart) plus per-subscription Core/Detail resource views with resource-group swim lanes. Encodes the Azure→ArchiMate element-type mapping, the synthetic-grouping rules, the Core/Detail view pattern, and the deterministic layout conventions. Use when the user wants to visualise, document, or diagram an Azure tenant, subscriptions, resource groups, or resources as ArchiMate. Pairs with the archimate-generation skill (serialisation) and the archimate-architect agent (ownership).

skills.name: azure-deploy
skills.description: Deploy to Azure targets: Bicep infrastructure-as-code, Container Apps and App Service, Key Vault secret references, and the `az` deploy runbook (containerapp up/update, App Service slot swap + rollback, OIDC federated `az login`). Builds on the shared /deploy methodology — does not re-document generic pipeline stages, Docker, SBOM, or quality gates. Platform-neutral by CI system; pair with /github-deploy or /gitlab-cicd for the pipeline wiring. Use when the user says "azure deploy", "bicep", "container apps", "app service", "key vault", "slot swap", or "az cli deploy".

skills.name: azure-naming
skills.description: Apply the TCS Azure resource naming convention (IAM "POL Naming Convention Cloud Services") when naming, creating, or reviewing any Azure resource — in Bicep/Terraform, in a design doc (BOM / target-architecture), in a PR review, or when reconciling an existing estate. Encodes the four-part {ResourceType}-{Project}-{Environment}[-{Instance}] pattern, the controlled abbreviation / project / environment token lists, the no-hyphen global variant, per-type charset/length constraints, and the AzGov + IAM-policy authority chain. If you are about to type an Azure resource name, run this skill first.

skills.name: business-process-modeling
skills.description: Model business processes using BPMN 2.0 notation to capture current-state (AS-IS) or future-state (TO-BE) workflows. Use when stakeholders need to understand a process before automating it, or when analysing process inefficiencies.

skills.name: change-request
skills.description: Draft, validate, and track a change request (RFC) through the CAB process. Use before any production change that could affect availability, data integrity, or security. Use when submitting a MOEX migration wave for CAB approval. Use when planning a rollout with a formal impact assessment and rollback plan.

skills.name: context-window-management
skills.description: Manage context window health in long-running agent sessions and multi-agent pipelines. Use when a session has been running for 30+ tool calls, when reasoning quality degrades, when sub-agents start forgetting earlier decisions, or when planning a MOEX migration run that will span multiple agents and sessions.

skills.name: deploy
skills.description: Shared, stack-agnostic CI/CD methodology: generic pipeline stages, secrets + OIDC principles, SHA/version pinning, validation and quality gates, multi-stage Docker/container builds, and SBOM generation. This is the common base every deployment skill builds on — for platform-specific procedure use /github-deploy (GitHub Actions), /azure-deploy (Azure targets), or /gitlab-cicd (GitLab). Use when the user says "deploy", "pipeline", "CI/CD", "Docker", "container build", "SBOM", or "release".

skills.name: deprecation-and-migration
skills.description: Migrate an existing API or integration from one auth/protocol/topology to another without breaking consumers. Use when deprecating an endpoint, changing auth schemes (e.g., Basic/header → OAuth2 JWT), adding a gateway layer, or retiring a version. Produces a migration guide, coexistence window plan, and a breaking-changes checklist.

skills.name: develop
skills.description: Implement a feature or fix a bug. Reads project context and team findings, plans the implementation, writes code following SOLID/Clean Code principles, performs a self-review, and reports changes with testing requirements. Use when the user says "implement", "build", "code", "fix", or "add feature".

skills.name: document
skills.description: Update project documentation to reflect recent changes. Updates CLAUDE.md, creates ADRs, consolidates team-findings.md, maintains domain and operations docs, and detects documentation drift. Use when the user says "update docs", "document", "update context", "ADR", or after other skills complete their work.

skills.name: documentation-build-order
skills.description: Sequences all documentation artefacts in the correct dependency order for a project. Selects a variant (greenfield, brownfield, single-feature, or strategy-only) and produces a build plan that chains the other skills (/requirements-analysis, /agile-user-stories, /enterprise-architecture-framework, /architecture-decision-records, /business-process-modeling, /use-case-modeling, /document) in the right order. Use when starting a new project, kicking off a major feature, or auditing whether documentation is complete and in the right sequence.

skills.name: draft-content
skills.description: Génère du contenu de communication interne TCS sur claude-agents — daily tip, weekly focus, monthly share, hackathon brief — prêt à publier sur Confluence ou Teams.

skills.name: enterprise-architecture-framework
skills.description: Apply enterprise architecture discipline: scope domains (Business/Application/Data/ Technology), produce architecture views, select the right ArchiMate elements, and manage governance gates. Use when designing system-wide structure, reviewing an architectural proposal, or mapping a solution to the enterprise landscape.

skills.name: eval-driven-development
skills.description: Define evaluation criteria and success metrics before writing any implementation code. Use when building a new capability, automating a pipeline, or validating a migration. Use before starting any MOEX API migration run to define what "correct migration" means. Use after discovering that a feature "works" but no one can prove it works repeatably.

skills.name: evaluate-contribution
skills.description: Évalue une contribution (agent/skill/team) avant ajout à la marketplace — compare avec l'existant, vérifie la qualité, recommande le bon type.

skills.name: github-deploy
skills.description: Author GitHub Actions deployment pipelines: workflow YAML and triggers, SHA-pinned actions, reusable workflows (`workflow_call`), matrix builds, GitHub Environments with protection rules and required reviewers, and OIDC federation to Azure (no stored service-principal secret). Builds on the shared /deploy methodology — does not re-document generic stages, Docker, SBOM, or quality gates. Use when the user says "github actions", "workflow", ".github/workflows", "reusable workflow", "github environment", or "actions OIDC".

skills.name: gitlab-cicd
skills.description: Author and structure GitLab CI/CD pipelines: `.gitlab-ci.yml` stages, `rules:`/`needs:` DAG scheduling, `include:`/`extends:` reuse, GROUP-vs-project CI/CD variables (masked/protected), GitLab Environments with manual approval gates, and container-registry auth via `CI_JOB_TOKEN`. Keeps a generic core; TCS specifics (git-scm.tcsgroup.ch, group tokens) are clearly-labelled examples. Use when the user says "gitlab ci", ".gitlab-ci.yml", "gitlab pipeline", "gitlab runner", "ci variables", or "gitlab environment".

skills.name: harness-engineering
skills.description: Design and instrument an autonomous execution loop (harness) for a multi-step agent task. Use when a pipeline must iterate until a condition is met, when a task needs automatic rollback on failure, or when a multi-agent workflow must be interrupted safely. Produces a harness specification with locked success metrics, exit conditions, and a rollback path.

skills.name: incident-response
skills.description: Manage a production incident from detection to blameless postmortem. Use when a production system is degraded or down, when an SLA is at risk, or when a critical alert fires and requires coordinated response. Use when drafting the postmortem after an incident is resolved.

skills.name: integration-patterns
skills.description: Identify, select, and document integration patterns for system-to-system communication. Covers messaging, routing, transformation, and reliability patterns aligned to EIP vocabulary. Use when designing how two or more systems exchange data, or when documenting an existing integration for review.

skills.name: knowledge-capture
skills.description: After completing a non-trivial task, extract the patterns, decisions, and gotchas discovered during the session into a durable knowledge file. Use at the end of a migration session, a complex debugging session, or any work that uncovered non-obvious constraints. The goal is to make the NEXT session faster by eliminating re-discovery.

skills.name: observability-and-instrumentation
skills.description: Instruments code so production behavior is visible and diagnosable. Use when adding logging, metrics, or tracing to an integration flow. Use when shipping any ACE/APIC flow to production. Use when an ELK alert fires and the root cause is not diagnosable from existing telemetry.

skills.name: pattern-diagram-design
skills.description: Generates TCS integration-pattern diagrams as Mermaid, aligned to the ARCHI catalog and Gregor Hohpe's Enterprise Integration Patterns (EIP) vocabulary. Use when the user wants to design, draw, document, or choose an integration pattern — sync/async APIs, proxies, channels, routing, file transfer (MFT/ETL/ELT), retry/replay, or a System-Process (SP) page — or names a backend (APIC/ACE/MQ, VTOM, Databricks) and asks how the integration should look. Mainly used by the architect (and the tester to derive test scenarios).

skills.name: postman-collection-generator
skills.description: Generates a ready-to-import Postman collection (v2.1 JSON) for non-regression API testing in IBM integration environments (APIC, ACE, MQ). Use when the user wants to create, generate, or build a Postman collection — or supplies an OpenAPI/Swagger spec, payload examples, Kibana traces, or API code to test, migrate, or validate.

skills.name: publish-confluence
skills.description: Publish a repository's documentation or presentation to Confluence as a navigable, read-only page tree using native ADF (via the Atlassian MCP). Mirrors the source structure as subpages, puts emoji in section titles, stamps the bundle version on the root title plus a generated banner on every page, renders diagrams as Mermaid (or Lucid embeds), wires cross-links, and is idempotent. Use when the user says "publish to Confluence", "update the wiki", or "publish the docs/presentation".

skills.name: publish-insights
skills.description: Publie un insight anonymisé dans docs/INSIGHTS.md et ouvre optionnellement une Issue GitLab backlog si l'insight suggère une amélioration de la librairie.

skills.name: quality-gates
skills.description: Wire and graduate CI quality gates for code, infrastructure-as-code, and docs. Covers self-hosted SonarQube (standalone sonar-scanner-cli + provisioned project key), Bicep build/lint, Checkov, and markdownlint, plus a non-blocking→blocking graduation path. Use when the user says "quality gate", "sonar", "sonarqube", "checkov", "bicep lint", or "code coverage gate".

skills.name: requirements-analysis
skills.description: Elicit, analyse, document, and validate software requirements. Produces a Requirements Specification (BRD/FRS) with uniquely identified, traceable, testable requirements. Use when starting a new feature, clarifying scope, or formalising stakeholder needs before architecture or development begins.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Review code for correctness, security, performance, and adherence to project standards. Checks for SOLID violations, OWASP vulnerabilities, architectural consistency, and IBM APIC/ACE integration patterns. Use when the user says "review", "check code", "code quality", or before merging a PR.

skills.name: runbook
skills.description: Write or execute an operational runbook for a specific system or procedure. Use when documenting a recurring operation (restart, failover, deployment rollback, queue drain). Use before executing a risky procedure to ensure every step has a verification check and rollback path. Use when onboarding a new team member to an operational procedure.

skills.name: security-audit
skills.description: Dedicated security audit covering OWASP Top 10, secret scanning, dependency CVEs, authentication/authorization, cryptography, and IBM integration security (APIC/ACE). More thorough than the security section in /review. Use when the user says "security audit", "pentest", "vulnerability scan", "OWASP", or before a production release.

skills.name: security-by-design
skills.description: Apply security-by-design principles during architecture and development: threat modelling, secure defaults, least privilege, defence in depth. Use when designing a new component, reviewing an existing design for security gaps, or producing a security section for an ADR or HLD.

skills.name: source-driven-development
skills.description: Grounds every implementation in official documentation before writing code. Use when working with any IBM API (APIC, ACE, MQ, DataPower), OpenAPI specs, or any framework where version-specific correctness matters. Use when reviewing integration patterns for deprecated or unofficial patterns.

skills.name: spec-review-with-rules
skills.description: Review an IBM APIC API spec (YAML/JSON) with path-specific rules before cutover. Use after migrating an API spec, after adding a new endpoint, or before promoting to production. Applies rules at four severity levels (critical, high, medium, low) and blocks deployment on critical findings.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Debug a failing system by following four ordered phases: root-cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and implementation with a failing test first. Use when a bug is not immediately obvious, when a previous fix attempt failed, or when the same type of error reappears in different places.

skills.name: test
skills.description: Run tests, analyze results, write missing tests, and report quality metrics. Checks infrastructure requirements, runs the suite, identifies coverage gaps, and optionally runs mutation testing. Use after /develop completes, or when the user says "test", "run tests", "validate", "coverage", or "check quality".

skills.name: use-case-modeling
skills.description: Identify actors and use cases, write use case specifications, and express acceptance criteria in Gherkin BDD format. Use when the system scope is being defined, or when test coverage needs to trace back to actor goals.

teams.name: claude-agents-maintainers
teams.description: Product-owner-assistant (lead) + Library-maintainer + Architect + DevOps — the only team that may modify this library.

teams.name: community-team
teams.description: Community-manager + Adoption-assistant + Best-practices — drive Claude Code adoption across TCS.

teams.name: feature-team
teams.description: Developer + Tester + Documentarian — implement a feature end-to-end.

teams.name: full-team
teams.description: All agents — major features, bootstrapping, migrations.

teams.name: moex-team
teams.description: Architect + Developer + Tester + Documentarian — IBM integration migrations (APIC, ACE, MQ).

teams.name: release-team
teams.description: DevOps + Tester + Documentarian — prepare and validate a release.

hooks.description: Full agent pipeline: signals the orchestrator when each agent finishes so the next one in the chain starts. Covers all 6 agents.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.SubagentStop' array below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: Auto-format Python files with black and ruff after every write or edit.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.PostToolUse' array below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: Run pytest automatically after every file write or edit. Keeps the agent honest — it sees test failures immediately rather than at commit time.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.PostToolUse' array below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: PreToolUse guard: warns before dangerous git commands execute.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.PreToolUse' array below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: Publish docs/presentation/index.html to Confluence automatically after every edit. Requires CONFLUENCE_* variables in the environment or a .env file at the repo root.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.PostToolUse' array below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: Auto-detect AGENT.md / SKILL.md files in the project that are not part of the claude-agents library. Fires on every user message. Prints a reminder for Claude to run the evaluate-contribution agent when unknown contributions are found. Never blocks — informational only.
install: 2. Merge the 'hooks.UserPromptSubmit' block below into your .claude/settings.json
3. Keep LIBRARY_AGENTS and LIBRARY_SKILLS in the script in sync with the claude-agents library

hooks.description: Desktop notification when Claude finishes a task. Linux: notify-send, macOS: osascript, Windows: msg.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.Stop' entry below into your .claude/settings.json

hooks.description: Block user prompts containing personal data (PII). Fires on every user message before it reaches Claude. Exit code 2 cancels the submission. Covers: AVS/AHV, IBAN, credit card, Swiss phone, e-mail, CHE company ID, passport/ID mentions, dates of birth, postal addresses, long numeric IDs.
install: 2. Merge the 'hooks.UserPromptSubmit' block below into your .claude/settings.json
3. Adjust the path in 'command' to match where you placed pii_check.py

hooks.description: Run bandit security scan after every Python file edit.
hooks.install: Merge the 'hooks.PostToolUse' array below into your .claude/settings.json

workflows.name: create-agent
workflows.description: Designs, writes, validates and opens a Merge Request for a new agent in the Agentic-Claude library. Use when a new specialized AI role is needed that has a distinct tool set, domain expertise, or strict boundary rules not covered by existing agents. Spawns: architect → developer → evaluate-contribution → library-maintainer.

workflows.name: create-skill
workflows.description: Writes, validates and opens a Merge Request for a new skill (slash command) in the Agentic-Claude library. Use when a repeatable workflow should be invocable as /skill-name in any Claude Code session. Spawns: developer → evaluate-contribution → library-maintainer.

workflows.name: feature-release
workflows.description: End-to-end pipeline: feature-team implements and tests a feature, then release-team validates, packages and delivers it. Two teams run sequentially, orchestrated by product-owner-assistant. Use when a feature is ready to ship in the same session.

workflows.name: onboard-project
workflows.description: Analyzes an existing project and produces a tailored Claude Code adoption plan. Installs the right agents, skills, and teams, then configures CLAUDE.md with project-specific context. Spawns: adoption-assistant → architect → documentarian.

workflows.name: release
workflows.description: Prepares and publishes a new library release: updates documentation, bumps the version, syncs Confluence and the interactive installer. Use after a significant batch of agents, skills or teams has been merged and stabilized. Spawns: documentarian → devops → library-maintainer.
